name: web-accessibility
description: Web accessibility compliance specialist. Use when conducting WCAG compliance
capabilities: Conduct WCAG 2.1/2.2 compliance audits at levels A, AA, and AAA
Analyze screen reader compatibility and semantic HTML structure
Validate keyboard navigation and focus management
Evaluate color contrast ratios against WCAG requirements
Review alternative text and media accessibility
Check form labels and error handling accessibility
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill containing only markdown documentation. The static analyzer flagged version numbers as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' and markdown code fences as 'shell backticks' - both are false positives. No executable code, network calls, file access, or command execution present. This skill provides AI assistants with accessibility expertise for WCAG compliance audits.
valueStatement: Web accessibility issues can exclude users with disabilities from accessing digital content. This skill provides comprehensive WCAG compliance audits, screen reader compatibility testing, and actionable remediation guidance for creating inclusive web experiences.
userTitle: Audit Websites for WCAG Accessibility Compliance
limitations: Cannot actually test live websites - provides guidance for manual and automated testing
Cannot run screen reader software or verify actual screen reader output
Cannot access external URLs or analyze deployed websites directly

useCases.title: Build Accessible Components
useCases.description: Learn to implement accessible HTML, ARIA attributes, and keyboard interactions in web components.

useCases.title: Conduct Accessibility Audits
useCases.description: Perform systematic accessibility testing including keyboard-only navigation and screen reader validation.

useCases.title: Design Inclusive Interfaces
useCases.description: Apply inclusive design principles and ensure color contrast meets WCAG standards.

promptTemplates.title: Basic WCAG Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this webpage for WCAG Level A compliance issues. Identify missing alt text, keyboard accessibility problems, and missing form labels.

promptTemplates.title: Color Contrast Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the color contrast ratios on this page. Flag any text that fails WCAG AA or AAA requirements and suggest better color combinations.

promptTemplates.title: Screen Reader Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this HTML for screen reader compatibility. Check semantic structure, ARIA labels, heading hierarchy, and landmark regions.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Accessibility Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive WCAG 2.2 AA compliance audit. Analyze color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, form accessibility, and multimedia alternatives. Provide specific code fixes.

bestPractices: Start with semantic HTML before adding ARIA attributes
Test keyboard navigation using only Tab, Enter, and Arrow keys
Verify all interactive elements have visible focus indicators
Include descriptive alt text for all meaningful images
antiPatterns: Fixing individual elements without considering overall page structure
Overusing ARIA when native HTML elements provide the behavior
Treating accessibility as a final checklist item rather than a design principle
Using color alone to convey information

faq.question: Which AI platforms is this skill compatible with?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code. It provides accessibility expertise to any AI assistant.

faq.question: What WCAG levels does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 standards at all three conformance levels: A (minimum), AA (recommended), and AAA (enhanced).

faq.question: Can this skill test live websites directly?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expert guidance and prompts for conducting accessibility audits. Actual testing requires browser tools or manual review.

faq.question: Does this skill store or transmit any data?
faq.answer: No, this is a prompt-based skill with no code execution. No data is collected, stored, or transmitted.

faq.question: How does this compare to automated accessibility tools?
faq.answer: Automated tools catch about 30 percent of accessibility issues. This skill helps identify contextual and complex issues that require human judgment.

faq.question: What should I do after an accessibility audit?
faq.answer: Prioritize fixes by severity, start with Level A issues, test changes with keyboard and screen readers, and document remediation steps.
